steps.title: Identify the key signal: the contrast and its meaning
steps.content: The passage presents a direct contrast immediately after explaining Barragán's travels: the José Mojica building's geometric, modern forms opposed to the Harper de Garibi House's “historically inspired architecture”—explicitly identified as an “early project.” This contrast is positioned as evidence of a stylistic evolution in his work.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice B identifies the passage's main argument: a shift in Barragán's aesthetic driven by his international exposure.

The passage demonstrates this through its contrast between his early historically inspired work (Harper de Garibi House) and his later modernist designs (José Mojica building), while the other choices mischaracterize the architectural styles or reverse the timeline.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
